RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_82.title: Overview
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_82.content:
High-performance distributed vector database written in Rust with:
- **HNSW indexing**: 61µs p50 latency, 16,400 QPS
- **Cypher queries**: Neo4j-compatible graph queries
- **39 attention mechanisms**: Flash, Linear, Graph, Hyperbolic
- **SONA**: Self-Optimizing Neural Architecture
- **Auto-sharding**: Consistent hashing, Raft consensus

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_114.title: Performance Benchmarks
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_114.content:
| Operation | Dimensions | Latency | Throughput |
|-----------|-----------|---------|-----------|
| HNSW Search (k=10) | 384 | 61µs | 16,400 QPS |
| Cosine Distance | 1536 | 143ns | 7M ops/sec |
| Batch Distance (1000) | 384 | 237µs | 4.2M/sec |

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_122.title: ⚠️ CRITICAL: VectorDB is MEMORY-RESIDENT Only
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_122.content:
**RuVector's VectorDB is in-memory only** - it has NO built-in persistence!

The VectorDB class only has these methods:
- `insert(id, vector, metadata)` - Add vector
- `insertBatch(entries)` - Batch add
- `search(query, k)` - HNSW search
- `get(id)` - Get by ID
- `delete(id)` - Remove
- `len()` / `isEmpty()` - Size queries

**There is NO `save()` or `load()` method!**

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_136.title: VectorDB Dimension Limit
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_136.content:
RuVector's native VectorDB supports **128 dimensions only**. For larger embeddings (like 384-dim MiniLM or 1536-dim OpenAI), you must:
1. Project down to 128 dims before HNSW indexing
2. Use the fallback brute-force search for larger dimensions

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_142.title: Three Learning Loops
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_142.content:
1. **Instant Loop** (<100μs): Per-request MicroLoRA adaptation
2. **Background Loop** (Hourly): K-means++ pattern extraction
3. **Deep Loop** (Weekly): EWC++ consolidation, dream memory

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_177.title: Storage Format
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_177.content:
```
.ruvector/mydb/
├── vectors.bin     # Binary vector data (Float32Array)
├── metadata.json   # ID index + metadata
├── manifest.json   # Version, dimensions, count
└── wal.log         # Write-ahead log
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_588.title: Project Setup - Local Installation (Recommended)
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_588.content:
Always install RuvNet packages **locally per project**, not globally:

```bash

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_599.title: Initialize claude-flow for the project
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_599.content:
npx claude-flow@alpha init --force
```

**Why Local?**
- **Isolation**: Each project has its own knowledge base and memory
- **Version Control**: Different projects can use different versions
- **No Pollution**: No cross-project data contamination
- **Reproducibility**: `package.json` defines exact dependencies

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_703.content:

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_712.title: Railway Deployment (Recommended for Production)
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_712.content:
Railway supports Node.js native modules and persistent storage:

```bash

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_724.title: Deploy
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_724.content:
railway up
```

**railway.json configuration:**
```json
{
  "build": {
    "builder": "NIXPACKS",
    "buildCommand": "npm install && npm run build"
  },
  "deploy": {
    "startCommand": "npm start",
    "healthcheckPath": "/health"
  }
}
```

**Key Railway Features:**
- ✅ Node.js v22 support
- ✅ Native module compilation (node-gyp)
- ✅ Persistent volumes for `.swarm/` and `.ruvector/`
- ✅ Auto-scaling on traffic
- ✅ Zero-config Node.js detection

**Mount persistent volume for data:**
```bash
railway volume create --mount /app/.swarm
railway volume create --mount /app/.ruvector
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_762.title: Install build tools for native modules
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_762.content:
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++

WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_762.section: Install build tools for native modules

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_772.title: Use Case: No External AI APIs Allowed
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_772.content: For companies where data cannot leave the network, use local LLMs:
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_772.section: Use Case: No External AI APIs Allowed

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_780.title: Persistent data directories
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_780.content:
VOLUME ["/app/.swarm", "/app/.ruvector"]

EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```

**docker-compose.yml:**
```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  app:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - swarm-data:/app/.swarm
      - ruvector-data:/app/.ruvector
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  swarm-data:
  ruvector-data:
```

**Run with:**
```bash
docker-compose up -d
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_812.title: Vercel Deployment (Limited - Read-Only Use Cases)
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_812.content:
**⚠️ Limitations:**
- Ephemeral storage (no persistent `.swarm/` or `.ruvector/`)
- Native module GLIBC compatibility issues
- 10-second function timeout (hobby), 60s (pro)
- No WebSocket support in serverless

**When Vercel Works:**
- Static sites with pre-built knowledge bases
- API routes that query external databases
- Read-only vector search (bundle data at build time)

**Workaround for read-only:**
```javascript
// Bundle knowledge at build time
import knowledgeBase from './data/knowledge.json';

// Create in-memory database from bundled data
const db = new ruvector.VectorDB(768);
knowledgeBase.forEach(item => db.insert(item.id, item.embedding));
```

**For Vercel, consider:**
- Use Vercel Postgres or Vercel KV for persistence
- Pre-compute embeddings at build time
- Use external vector DB (Pinecone, Weaviate Cloud)

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_984.title: Security Considerations for Enterprise
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_984.content:
1. **Network Isolation**: Run Ollama on internal network only
2. **No Telemetry**: Ollama and Qwen don't phone home
3. **Audit Logging**: Log all queries for compliance
4. **Data Encryption**: Encrypt `.swarm/` and `.ruvector/` at rest
5. **Access Control**: Use reverse proxy with authentication

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1006.title: Vercel GLIBC Errors
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1006.content:
**Problem:** `Error: /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6: version 'GLIBC_2.28' not found`

**Solution:** Use Railway or Docker instead, or bundle pre-computed data.

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1084.title: Description
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1084.content: RvLite is an SQLite-style vector database for browsers and edge environments. It provides a unified SQL + SPARQL + Cypher query interface with zero dependencies, running anywhere JavaScript runs - browsers, Node.js, Deno, Bun, Cloudflare Workers, and Vercel Edge.

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1097.title: Core Class: `RvLite`
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1097.content:
```javascript
import { RvLite } from '@rvlite/wasm';

// Create database instance
const db = await RvLite.create(options);
```

**Constructor Options:**
| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `path` | string | `:memory:` | Database file path |
| `dimensions` | number | 384 | Default vector dimensions |
| `indexType` | string | `hnsw` | Index type (hnsw, ivfflat) |
| `m` | number | 16 | HNSW connections per layer |
| `efConstruction` | number | 64 | HNSW construction quality |

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1168.title: Cypher Interface
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1168.content:
```javascript
// Graph pattern matching
const results = await db.cypher(`
  MATCH (d:Document)-[:SIMILAR]->(related:Document)
  WHERE d.id = $docId
  RETURN related.content, related.score
  ORDER BY related.score DESC
  LIMIT 5
`, { docId: 123 });

// Create relationships
await db.cypher(`
  MATCH (a:Document), (b:Document)
  WHERE a.id = $aId AND b.id = $bId
  CREATE (a)-[:SIMILAR {score: $score}]->(b)
`, { aId: 1, bId: 2, score: 0.95 });
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1537.title: ruvector-pg.yaml
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1537.content:
server:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
  database: vectors

extension:
  version: "0.2.6"
  simd: auto  # auto | avx512 | avx2 | neon | none

indexes:
  hnsw:
    m: 16
    ef_construction: 64
    ef_search: 100
  ivfflat:
    lists: 100
    probes: 10

learning:
  enabled: true
  trajectory_retention: 30d
  pattern_threshold: 0.7

embeddings:
  default_model: all-MiniLM-L6-v2
  cache_size: 10000
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1853.title: Configuration Options
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_1853.content:
```javascript
const config = {
  // Storage
  path: './agent-memory.db',
  persistToDisk: true,

  // Vectors
  dimensions: 384,
  indexType: 'hnsw',  // hnsw | flat | ivf

  // HNSW Configuration
  hnswM: 16,              // Connections per layer
  hnswEf: 100,            // Dynamic candidate list size
  hnswEfConstruction: 64, // Build-time quality

  // Quantization
  quantization: 'scalar', // none | binary | scalar | product

  // Performance
  cacheSize: 1000,        // LRU cache entries
  batchSize: 100,         // Batch operation size

  // Learning
  learningEnabled: false,
  learningAlgorithm: 'decision-transformer',

  // ReasoningBank
  reasoningEnabled: false,
  reasoningConfidenceThreshold: 0.7,

  // Distributed
  quicSyncEnabled: false,
  syncEndpoint: null
};
```

**Environment Variables:**
```bash
AGENTDB_ENABLED=true
AGENTDB_PATH=.agentdb/memory.db
AGENTDB_QUANTIZATION=scalar  # binary|scalar|product|none
AGENTDB_CACHE_SIZE=1000
AGENTDB_HNSW_M=16
AGENTDB_HNSW_EF=100
AGENTDB_LEARNING=false
AGENTDB_LEARNING_ALGORITHM=decision-transformer
AGENTDB_REASONING=false
AGENTDB_QUIC_SYNC=false
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_2291.content: Self-learning synthetic data generator for AI/ML applications. Generates realistic synthetic data at scale using neural architecture that learns from patterns, supporting 37+ data types across multiple domains including healthcare, finance, and e-commerce.

| Location | `address`, `city`, `state`, `country`, `zipCode`, `latitude`, `longitude` |

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_2746.title: Overview
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_2746.content: RuVector implements automatic tiered storage similar to a computer's memory hierarchy. Frequently accessed vectors stay in fast storage while rarely used vectors are automatically compressed to save memory.

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_2760.title: How It Works
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_2760.content:
```sql
-- Vectors are automatically promoted/demoted based on access patterns
-- No manual configuration required

-- Example: Insert vector (starts in Hot tier)
INSERT INTO documents (content, embedding)
VALUES ('New document', '[0.1, 0.2, ...]'::ruvector);

-- As access frequency decreases, vector moves to cooler tiers
-- Query performance remains excellent due to intelligent caching
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_2776.title: Format Specifications
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_2776.content:
**f32 (Hot)**
- Full IEEE 754 single-precision floats
- 4 bytes per dimension
- Zero compression overhead
- Maximum precision for frequently accessed vectors

**f16 (Warm)**
- IEEE 754 half-precision floats
- 2 bytes per dimension
- 50% memory reduction
- Imperceptible accuracy loss for most use cases

**PQ8 (Cool)**
- Product Quantization with 8-bit centroids
- 256 centroids per subspace
- ~97% accuracy retention
- 8x memory reduction

**PQ4 (Cold)**
- Product Quantization with 4-bit centroids
- 16 centroids per subspace
- ~95% accuracy retention
- 16x memory reduction

**Binary (Archive)**
- 1-bit per dimension
- Hamming distance for initial filtering
- Re-ranking with full precision on final candidates
- 32x memory reduction

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_3051.title: Semantic Search with PostgreSQL
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_3051.content:
```sql
-- Search patterns by semantic similarity
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION search_patterns_semantic(
    query_embedding ruvector,
    domain_filter TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
    min_confidence FLOAT8 DEFAULT 0.5,
    result_limit INT DEFAULT 10
) RETURNS TABLE (
    key TEXT,
    content TEXT,
    confidence FLOAT8,
    similarity FLOAT8
) AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
        p.key,
        p.content,
        p.confidence,
        1 - (pe.embedding <=> query_embedding) AS similarity
    FROM patterns p
    JOIN pattern_embeddings pe ON p.id = pe.pattern_id
    WHERE p.confidence >= min_confidence
      AND (domain_filter IS NULL OR p.domain = domain_filter)
    ORDER BY pe.embedding <=> query_embedding
    LIMIT result_limit;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_3090.title: docker-compose.yml
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_3090.content:
version: '3.8'

services:
  ruvector-postgres:
    image: ruvnet/ruvector-postgres:latest
    container_name: ruvector-db
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ruvector
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: vectors
      # Extension configuration
      RUVECTOR_SIMD: auto  # auto | avx512 | avx2 | neon
      RUVECTOR_HNSW_M: 16
      RUVECTOR_HNSW_EF: 100
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - ruvector_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./init:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ruvector -d vectors"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          memory: 2G

volumes:
  ruvector_data:
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_3126.title: Initialization Script

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_3157.title: docker-compose.prod.yml
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_3157.content:
version: '3.8'

services:
  ruvector-postgres:
    image: ruvnet/ruvector-postgres:latest
    deploy:
      replicas: 1
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
    secrets:
      - db_password
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    networks:
      - ruvector-net

  agentdb:
    image: ruvnet/agentdb:latest
    deploy:
      replicas: 2
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://ruvector@ruvector-postgres:5432/vectors
      AGENTDB_QUANTIZATION: scalar
    depends_on:
      - ruvector-postgres
    networks:
      - ruvector-net

  flow-nexus:
    image: ruvnet/flow-nexus:latest
    deploy:
      replicas: 1
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://ruvector@ruvector-postgres:5432/vectors
      E2B_API_KEY_FILE: /run/secrets/e2b_key
    secrets:
      - e2b_key
    depends_on:
      - ruvector-postgres
      - agentdb
    networks:
      - ruvector-net

  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf
      - ./certs:/etc/nginx/certs
    depends_on:
      - flow-nexus
    networks:
      - ruvector-net

secrets:
  db_password:
    external: true
  e2b_key:
    external: true

volumes:
  pgdata:

networks:
  ruvector-net:
    driver: overlay
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_3233.title: k8s/ruvector-deployment.yaml
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_3233.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ruvector-postgres
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: ruvector-postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: ruvector-postgres
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: ruvector-postgres
          image: ruvnet/ruvector-postgres:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: ruvector-secrets
                  key: db-password
          volumeMounts:
            - name: pgdata
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "2Gi"
              cpu: "1000m"
            limits:
              memory: "4Gi"
              cpu: "2000m"
      volumes:
        - name: pgdata
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: ruvector-pvc

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: ruvector-postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app: ruvector-postgres
  ports:
    - port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
  type: ClusterIP
```

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_2.title: Overview
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_2.content:
AgentDB is a frontier vector database designed for AI agent memory systems. It combines vector search with causal reasoning, reinforcement learning, and explainable AI to enable agents to learn from experience, reason about causality, and provide traceable decisions.

**Version**: 1.6.1
**NPM Package**: `agentdb`
**License**: MIT

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_435.title: Core Vector DB Operations (5 tools)
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_435.content:
| Tool | Description | Parameters |
|------|-------------|------------|
| `agentdb_init` | Initialize database with schema and optimizations | `db_path?: string`, `reset?: boolean` |
| `agentdb_insert` | Insert single vector with auto-generated embedding | `text: string`, `metadata?: object`, `session_id?: string`, `tags?: string[]` |
| `agentdb_insert_batch` | Batch insert with parallel embedding generation | `items: array`, `batch_size?: number` |
| `agentdb_search` | Semantic k-NN search with cosine similarity | `query: string`, `k?: number`, `min_similarity?: number`, `filters?: object` |
| `agentdb_delete` | Delete vectors by ID or filters | `id?: number`, `filters?: object` |

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_445.title: Frontier Memory Features (9 tools)
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_445.content:
| Tool | Description | Parameters |
|------|-------------|------------|
| `reflexion_store` | Store episode with self-critique | `session_id: string`, `task: string`, `reward: number`, `success: boolean`, `critique?: string`, `input?: string`, `output?: string` |
| `reflexion_retrieve` | Retrieve relevant past episodes | `task: string`, `k?: number`, `only_failures?: boolean`, `only_successes?: boolean`, `min_reward?: number` |
| `skill_create` | Create reusable skill | `name: string`, `description: string`, `code?: string`, `success_rate?: number` |
| `skill_search` | Search skills by semantic similarity | `task: string`, `k?: number`, `min_success_rate?: number` |
| `causal_add_edge` | Add causal relationship | `cause: string`, `effect: string`, `uplift: number`, `confidence?: number`, `sample_size?: number` |
| `causal_query` | Query causal effects | `cause?: string`, `effect?: string`, `min_confidence?: number`, `min_uplift?: number`, `limit?: number` |
| `recall_with_certificate` | Retrieve with causal utility and provenance | `query: string`, `k?: number`, `alpha?: number`, `beta?: number`, `gamma?: number` |
| `learner_discover` | Auto-discover causal patterns | `min_attempts?: number`, `min_success_rate?: number`, `min_confidence?: number`, `dry_run?: boolean` |
| `db_stats` | Get database statistics | (none) |

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_459.title: Learning System Tools (10 tools)
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_459.content:
| Tool | Description | Parameters |
|------|-------------|------------|
| `learning_start_session` | Start RL session with algorithm | `user_id: string`, `session_type: string`, `config: object` |
| `learning_end_session` | End session and save policy | `session_id: string` |
| `learning_predict` | Get AI action recommendation | `session_id: string`, `state: string` |
| `learning_feedback` | Submit learning feedback | `session_id: string`, `state: string`, `action: string`, `reward: number`, `success: boolean`, `next_state?: string` |
| `learning_train` | Train RL policy | `session_id: string`, `epochs?: number`, `batch_size?: number`, `learning_rate?: number` |
| `learning_metrics` | Get learning performance metrics | `session_id?: string`, `time_window_days?: number`, `include_trends?: boolean`, `group_by?: string` |
| `learning_transfer` | Transfer learning between sessions | `source_session?: string`, `target_session?: string`, `source_task?: string`, `target_task?: string`, `min_similarity?: number`, `transfer_type?: string` |
| `learning_explain` | Explain action recommendations | `query: string`, `k?: number`, `explain_depth?: string`, `include_confidence?: boolean`, `include_evidence?: boolean`, `include_causal?: boolean` |
| `experience_record` | Record tool execution experience | `session_id: string`, `tool_name: string`, `action: string`, `outcome: string`, `reward: number`, `success: boolean` |
| `reward_signal` | Calculate reward signal | `success: boolean`, `episode_id?: number`, `target_achieved?: boolean`, `efficiency_score?: number`, `quality_score?: number` |

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_474.content:
| Tool | Description | Parameters |
|------|-------------|------------|
| `agentdb_stats` | Comprehensive database statistics | `detailed?: boolean` |
| `agentdb_pattern_store` | Store reasoning pattern | `taskType: string`, `approach: string`, `successRate: number`, `tags?: string[]`, `metadata?: object` |
| `agentdb_pattern_search` | Search patterns semantically | `task: string`, `k?: number`, `threshold?: number`, `filters?: object` |
| `agentdb_pattern_stats` | Get pattern statistics | (none) |
| `agentdb_clear_cache` | Clear query cache | `cache_type?: string` |

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_490.title: 1. Q-Learning
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_490.content:
- **Type**: Model-free, off-policy
- **Update Rule**: Q(s,a) = Q(s,a) + α[r + γ max_a' Q(s',a') - Q(s,a)]
- **Best For**: Discrete state/action spaces, simple environments

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_495.title: 2. SARSA (State-Action-Reward-State-Action)
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_495.content:
- **Type**: Model-free, on-policy
- **Update Rule**: Q(s,a) = Q(s,a) + α[r + γ Q(s',a') - Q(s,a)]
- **Best For**: Safety-critical applications where exploration must be conservative

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_500.title: 3. DQN (Deep Q-Network)
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_500.content:
- **Type**: Model-free, off-policy with neural network
- **Features**: Experience replay, target networks
- **Best For**: High-dimensional state spaces, visual inputs

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_505.title: 4. Policy Gradient
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_505.content:
- **Type**: Model-free, on-policy
- **Update Rule**: ∇J(θ) = E[∇log π(a|s;θ) * G]
- **Best For**: Continuous action spaces, direct policy optimization

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_510.title: 5. Actor-Critic
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_510.content:
- **Type**: Hybrid value/policy method
- **Components**: Actor (policy), Critic (value function)
- **Best For**: Balancing variance reduction with policy optimization

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_515.title: 6. PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization)
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_515.content:
- **Type**: Model-free, on-policy with clipped updates
- **Objective**: L^CLIP(θ) = E[min(r_t(θ)A_t, clip(r_t(θ), 1-ε, 1+ε)A_t)]
- **Best For**: Stable training, large-scale applications

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_520.title: 7. Decision Transformer
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_520.content:
- **Type**: Sequence modeling approach to RL
- **Architecture**: GPT-style transformer conditioned on returns
- **Best For**: Offline RL, leveraging large-scale pretraining

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_525.title: 8. MCTS (Monte Carlo Tree Search)
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_525.content:
- **Type**: Model-based planning
- **Steps**: Selection, Expansion, Simulation, Backpropagation
- **Best For**: Game playing, planning with known models

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_530.title: 9. Model-Based RL
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_530.content:
- **Type**: Learns environment dynamics model
- **Approach**: Learn P(s'|s,a), then plan using learned model
- **Best For**: Sample-efficient learning, environments with clear dynamics

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_535.title: Configuration Options
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_535.content:
```typescript
interface LearningConfig {
  learningRate: number;            // α - step size (0.001 - 0.1)
  discountFactor: number;          // γ - future reward discount (0.9 - 0.99)
  explorationRate?: number;        // ε - exploration vs exploitation (0.1)
  batchSize?: number;              // Mini-batch size for training (32)
  targetUpdateFrequency?: number;  // Steps between target network updates (100)
}
```

---

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_549.title: HNSW Indexing Configuration
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_549.content: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) provides 10-100x speedup over brute-force search.

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_553.title: Configuration Parameters
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_553.content:
| Parameter | Default | Range | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------|-------------|
| `M` | 16 | 4-64 | Max connections per layer. Higher = better recall, more memory |
| `efConstruction` | 200 | 100-500 | Candidate list during construction. Higher = better index quality |
| `efSearch` | 100 | 50-500 | Candidate list during search. Higher = better recall, slower search |
| `metric` | 'cosine' | cosine, l2, ip | Distance metric |
| `dimension` | 1536 | 128-4096 | Vector dimension (must match embeddings) |
| `maxElements` | 100000 | 1000-10M | Maximum vectors in index |
| `persistIndex` | true | - | Save index to disk for persistence |
| `rebuildThreshold` | 0.1 | 0.05-0.5 | Rebuild when updates exceed this % |

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_566.title: Performance Characteristics
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_566.content:
- **Build Time**: O(N * log(N) * M * efConstruction)
- **Search Time**: O(log(N) * efSearch)
- **Memory**: O(N * M * dimension * 4 bytes)
- **Recall@10**: 95-99% with default settings

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_573.title: Distance Metrics
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_573.content:
| Metric | Formula | Use Case |
|--------|---------|----------|
| `cosine` | 1 - (a·b)/(||a|| * ||b||) | Text embeddings, normalized vectors |
| `l2` (Euclidean) | sqrt(Σ(a_i - b_i)²) | Image features, spatial data |
| `ip` (Inner Product) | -a·b | Maximum inner product search |

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_581.title: Similarity Conversion
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_581.content:
```typescript
switch (metric) {
  case 'cosine':    return 1 - distance;        // Distance to similarity
  case 'l2':        return Math.exp(-distance);  // Exponential decay
  case 'ip':        return -distance;            // Negate for similarity
}
```

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_663.title: Reflexion Memory Patterns
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_663.content: Based on the paper "Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning."

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_667.title: Core Concept
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_667.content:
Reflexion enables agents to learn from past experiences through:
1. **Task Execution**: Agent attempts a task
2. **Self-Critique**: Agent reflects on performance
3. **Memory Storage**: Episode stored with embedding
4. **Future Retrieval**: Similar past experiences inform new attempts

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_675.title: Episode Structure
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_675.content:
```typescript
interface Episode {
  id?: number;
  ts?: number;              // Timestamp
  sessionId: string;        // Session identifier
  task: string;             // Task description
  input?: string;           // Task input
  output?: string;          // Task output/result
  critique?: string;        // Self-reflection/critique
  reward: number;           // Performance score (0-1)
  success: boolean;         // Binary success indicator
  latencyMs?: number;       // Execution time
  tokensUsed?: number;      // Token consumption
  tags?: string[];          // Categorization tags
  metadata?: Record<string, any>;  // Additional context
}
```

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_758.title: Core Concept
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_758.content:
The Skill Library enables agents to:
1. **Extract Skills**: Convert successful episodes into reusable skills
2. **Semantic Search**: Find applicable skills for new tasks
3. **Composition**: Build complex behaviors from simpler skills
4. **Continuous Learning**: Update skills based on performance

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_833.title: Skill Score Calculation
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_833.content:
```typescript
// Composite score for skill ranking
const score =
  skill.similarity * 0.4 +           // Semantic relevance
  skill.successRate * 0.3 +          // Historical success
  Math.min(skill.uses / 1000, 1.0) * 0.1 +  // Experience factor
  skill.avgReward * 0.2;             // Average quality
```

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1021.title: Database Schema
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1021.content: AgentDB uses SQLite with the following core tables:

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1025.title: Episodes Table
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1025.content:
```sql
CREATE TABLE episodes (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  ts INTEGER DEFAULT (strftime('%s', 'now')),
  session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  task TEXT NOT NULL,
  input TEXT,
  output TEXT,
  critique TEXT,
  reward REAL NOT NULL,
  success INTEGER NOT NULL,
  latency_ms INTEGER,
  tokens_used INTEGER,
  tags TEXT,
  metadata TEXT
);
```

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1044.title: Skills Table
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1044.content:
```sql
CREATE TABLE skills (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  ts INTEGER DEFAULT (strftime('%s', 'now')),
  name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  signature TEXT,
  code TEXT,
  success_rate REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
  uses INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  avg_reward REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
  avg_latency_ms REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
  tags TEXT,
  metadata TEXT
);
```

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1062.title: Causal Edges Table
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1062.content:
```sql
CREATE TABLE causal_edges (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  ts INTEGER DEFAULT (strftime('%s', 'now')),
  from_memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  from_memory_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  to_memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  to_memory_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  similarity REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
  uplift REAL NOT NULL,
  confidence REAL DEFAULT 0.95,
  sample_size INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  evidence_ids TEXT,
  experiment_ids TEXT,
  confounder_score REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
  mechanism TEXT,
  metadata TEXT
);
```

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1083.title: Learning Sessions Table
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1083.content:
```sql
CREATE TABLE learning_sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  session_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  config TEXT NOT NULL,
  start_time INTEGER NOT NULL,
  end_time INTEGER,
  status TEXT NOT NULL,
  metadata TEXT
);
```

AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1097.title: Reasoning Patterns Table
AGENTDB_V1.6.1_KNOWLEDGE_BASE.md_1097.content:
```sql
CREATE TABLE reasoning_patterns (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  ts INTEGER DEFAULT (strftime('%s', 'now')),
  task_type TEXT NOT NULL,
  approach TEXT NOT NULL,
  success_rate REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.0,
  uses INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  avg_reward REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
  tags TEXT,
  metadata TEXT
);
```

AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_23.title: Overview
AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_23.content:
Agentic Flow is a production-ready AI agent orchestration platform featuring:

- **150+ specialized agents** across development, swarm coordination, GitHub, and more
- **213 MCP tools** across 4 servers (Claude Flow, Flow Nexus, Agentic Payments, SDK)
- **ReasoningBank** - Persistent learning memory system (46% faster, 100% success rate)
- **Agent Booster** - 352x faster local code transformations via Rust/WASM
- **Multi-Model Router** - 85-99% cost savings across 10+ LLM providers

| SWE-Bench Solve Rate | 84.8% |
| Token Reduction | 32.3% |
| Speed Improvement | 2.8-4.4x |
| Neural Models | 27+ |
| Agent Booster Speedup | 352x |
| Cost Savings (Router) | 85-99% |
| ReasoningBank Improvement | 46% faster |

- **Cost**: $0 (100% free local execution)

AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_684.title: Routing Modes
AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_684.content:
| Mode | Description | Use Case |
|------|-------------|----------|
| `manual` | User explicitly selects provider/model | Testing |
| `cost-optimized` | Automatically select cheapest suitable provider | Production |
| `performance-optimized` | Prioritize fastest provider | Real-time |
| `quality-optimized` | Prioritize most capable model | Critical tasks |
| `rule-based` | Use custom routing rules | Custom logic |

AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_733.title: 1. Single File Edit
AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_733.content:
```bash
npx agentic-flow agent-booster edit \
  --file src/api.ts \
  --instructions "Add error handling" \
  --code '...'
```

AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_746.title: 3. Markdown Parse (LLM-Compatible)
AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_746.content:
```markdown
filepath=src/user.ts instruction="Add type annotation"
\`\`\`typescript
const user: User = { id: string; name: string; };
\`\`\`
```

AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_757.title: agent-booster.yaml
AGENTIC-FLOW-V2-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_757.content:
agent_booster:
  max_concurrent_edits: 10
  batch_size: 100
  backup_files: true
  dry_run: false
  validate_syntax: true
  run_formatter: true
  log_level: "info"
```

FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_23.title: 1.1 Core Platform Components
FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_23.content:
Flow-Nexus is a cloud-based MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that provides:

- **Multi-Agent Swarm Orchestration** - Distributed agent coordination with multiple topologies
- **E2B Cloud Sandboxes** - Secure code execution environments
- **Neural Network Training** - DIY neural training with WASM SIMD acceleration
- **Gamification System** - rUv credits, challenges, achievements, and leaderboards
- **Supabase Backend** - Auth, database, real-time subscriptions, and storage

FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_33.title: 1.2 Technology Stack
FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_33.content:
| Component | Technology | Purpose |
|-----------|------------|---------|
| MCP Server | `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` | Tool protocol implementation |
| Database | Supabase PostgreSQL | Persistent storage, RLS security |
| Authentication | Supabase Auth | User management, sessions |
| Sandboxes | E2B | Cloud code execution |
| Real-time | Supabase Realtime | Live updates, subscriptions |
| Storage | Supabase Storage | File management |
| Payments | Stripe (via Edge Functions) | Credit purchases |
| Neural Ops | WASM SIMD | High-performance ML operations |

FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_16.content:
Check current credit balance and auto-refill status.

```json
{
  "name": "check_balance",
  "inputSchema": { "properties": {} }
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "balance": 150,
  "auto_refill_enabled": true,
  "auto_refill_threshold": 20,
  "auto_refill_amount": 50,
  "low_balance_warning": false
}
```

3.1 Supported Topologies
FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_17.content:
| Topology | Description | Best For |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| **hierarchical** | Tree structure with coordinator at root | Complex multi-stage tasks |
| **mesh** | Fully connected peer-to-peer | Collaborative parallel processing |
| **ring** | Circular chain of agents | Sequential pipeline workflows |
| **star** | Central hub with spoke agents | Centralized coordination tasks |

FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_17.title: 3.2 Agent Types

| **coordinator** | Orchestrates other agents | Task assignment, monitoring, aggregation |
| **researcher** | Information gathering | Search, analysis, summarization |
| **coder** | Code generation/modification | Programming, debugging, refactoring |
| **analyst** | Data analysis | Pattern recognition, metrics, insights |
| **optimizer** | Performance tuning | Optimization, benchmarking, efficiency |
| **worker** | General task execution | Flexible task handling |

FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_21.title: 7.3 Authentication Flow
FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_21.content:
```
1. user_register -> Creates Supabase auth user + profile
2. user_login -> Returns JWT session token
3. Token stored in FLOW_NEXUS_SESSION env var
4. All MCP tools validate session via getSessionContext()
5. RLS policies enforce data isolation
```

FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_21.title: 7.4 Security Features
FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_21.content:
- **PCI DSS SAQ-A Compliant** - No card data stored locally
- **Stripe Keys in Vault** - API keys stored in Supabase vault
- **Row-Level Security (RLS)** - Database-level access control
- **JWT Sessions** - Secure token-based authentication
- **Edge Functions** - Sensitive operations isolated
- **Rate Limiting** - Daily limits on free tier operations

FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_22.title: 8.3 Environment Variables
FLOW-NEXUS-v0.1.128-KNOWLEDGE-BASE.md_22.content:
| Variable | Description | Required |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| `SUPABASE_URL` | Supabase project URL | Yes |
| `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | Supabase anonymous key | Yes |
| `E2B_API_KEY` | E2B sandbox API key | For sandboxes |
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Anthropic API key | For Seraphina |
| `FLOW_NEXUS_SESSION` | Persisted user session | For auth |
| `FLOW_NEXUS_MODE` | Operating mode | No (default: complete) |
| `NODE_ENV` | Environment (test/production) | No |

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_59.title: Installation & Server Management
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_59.content:
| Command | Description | Example |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `ruvector-pg install` | Install PostgreSQL + RuVector | `ruvector-pg install --method docker` |
| `ruvector-pg status` | Check installation status | `ruvector-pg status` |
| `ruvector-pg start` | Start the database server | `ruvector-pg start` |
| `ruvector-pg stop` | Stop the database server | `ruvector-pg stop` |
| `ruvector-pg logs` | View server logs | `ruvector-pg logs --follow` |
| `ruvector-pg psql` | Connect with psql | `ruvector-pg psql "SELECT 1;"` |
| `ruvector-pg info` | Show extension info | `ruvector-pg info` |
| `ruvector-pg uninstall` | Remove installation | `ruvector-pg uninstall` |

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_72.title: Install Command Options
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_72.content:
```bash
ruvector-pg install [options]

Options:
  -m, --method <type>     Installation method: docker, native, auto (default: "auto")
  -p, --port <number>     PostgreSQL port (default: "5432")
  -u, --user <name>       Database user (default: "ruvector")
  --password <pass>       Database password (default: "ruvector")
  -d, --database <name>   Database name (default: "ruvector")
  --data-dir <path>       Persistent data directory (Docker only)
  --name <name>           Container name (default: "ruvector-postgres")
  --version <version>     RuVector version (default: "0.2.5")
  --pg-version <version>  PostgreSQL version for native (14, 15, 16, 17)
  --skip-postgres         Skip PostgreSQL installation (use existing)
  --skip-rust             Skip Rust installation (use existing)
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_120.title: List available types
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_120.content:
ruvector-pg attention list-types
```

**Available Attention Types**:
- Core: `scaled_dot_product_attention`, `multi_head_attention`, `flash_attention`
- Sparse: `sparse_attention`, `local_attention`, `strided_attention`, `random_attention`, `longformer_attention`
- Memory: `memory_attention`, `compressive_attention`, `memory_compressed_attention`
- Cross-Modal: `cross_attention`, `cross_modal_attention`, `multimodal_attention`
- Efficient: `linear_attention`, `performer_attention`, `reformer_attention`, `synthesizer_attention`
- Positional: `relative_attention`, `rotary_attention`, `alibi_attention`, `rope_attention`
- Graph: `graph_attention`, `gat_attention`, `sparse_graph_attention`
- Advanced: `self_attention`, `causal_attention`, `bidirectional_attention`, `grouped_query_attention`

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_143.title: List GNN types
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_143.content:
ruvector-pg gnn list-types
```

**GNN Layer Types**:
| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `gcn` | Graph Convolutional Network - Spectral graph convolutions using Chebyshev polynomials |
| `graphsage` | GraphSAGE - Inductive learning with neighborhood sampling and aggregation |
| `gat` | Graph Attention Network - Attention-weighted message passing between nodes |
| `gin` | Graph Isomorphism Network - Provably as powerful as WL-test |

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_158.title: Execute Cypher query
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_158.content: ruvector-pg graph query "MATCH (n) RETURN n LIMIT 10"

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_170.title: Traverse graph
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_170.content:
ruvector-pg graph traverse --start 1 --depth 3 --type bfs
```

**Cypher Query Examples**:
```cypher
-- Return first 10 nodes
MATCH (n) RETURN n LIMIT 10

-- Find all Person nodes
MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n

-- Find relationships
MATCH (a)-[r]->(b) RETURN a,r,b

-- Find by property
MATCH (n {name: 'Alice'}) RETURN n

-- Variable-length path
MATCH p=(a)-[*1..3]->(b) RETURN p

-- Create a node
CREATE (n:Person {name: 'Bob'}) RETURN n
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_288.title: Clear all agents
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_288.content:
ruvector-pg routing clear
```

**Optimization Targets**:
- `cost` - Minimize request cost
- `latency` - Minimize response time
- `quality` - Maximize output quality
- `balanced` - Balance all factors (default)

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_340.title: Get stats
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_340.content:
ruvector-pg quantization stats
```

**Quantization Methods**:
| Method | Bits/Dim | Compression | Accuracy Loss | Speed Boost |
|--------|----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|
| Binary (BQ) | 1 | 32x | ~20-30% | ~10-20x |
| Scalar (SQ8) | 8 | 4x | ~1-5% | ~2-4x |
| Product (PQ) | Variable | 8-32x | ~5-15% | ~5-10x |

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_734.title: Docker Compose
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_734.content:
```yaml
version: '3.8'

services:
  ruvector-postgres:
    image: ruvnet/ruvector-postgres:latest
    container_name: ruvector-pg
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ruvector
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-secret}
      POSTGRES_DB: ruvector
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - ruvector_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ruvector -d ruvector"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  ruvector_data:
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_777.title: Tiered Compression Architecture
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_777.content: RuVector implements a 5-tier compression architecture for optimal storage and performance:

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_781.title: Tier Overview
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_781.content:
| Tier | Name | Format | Compression | Memory Reduction | Use Case |
|------|------|--------|-------------|------------------|----------|
| 0 | **Hot** | f32 | None | 1x | Active queries, real-time search |
| 1 | **Warm** | f16 | 2x | 2x | Recently accessed data |
| 2 | **Cool** | int8 (SQ8) | 4x | 4x | Infrequent access |
| 3 | **Cold** | int4 | 8x | 8x | Archive, batch processing |
| 4 | **Archive** | Binary | 32x | 32x | Long-term storage, coarse filtering |

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_791.title: Configuration
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_791.content:
```sql
-- Enable tiered compression for a table
SELECT ruvector_enable_tiering(
  table_name::text,
  hot_retention::interval DEFAULT '1 hour',
  warm_retention::interval DEFAULT '1 day',
  cool_retention::interval DEFAULT '7 days',
  cold_retention::interval DEFAULT '30 days'
);

-- Manual tier migration
SELECT ruvector_migrate_tier(table_name::text, tier::int);

-- Get tier statistics
SELECT ruvector_tier_stats(table_name::text);
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_812.title: Binary Quantization (BQ)
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_812.content:
```sql
-- Convert vector to binary (sign-based)
SELECT binary_quantize_arr(vector::real[]);
-- Result: 1 bit per dimension, 32x compression
-- Use: Fast pre-filtering, approximate search
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_820.title: Scalar Quantization (SQ8)
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_820.content:
```sql
-- Convert vector to 8-bit integers
SELECT scalar_quantize_arr(vector::real[]);
-- Result: 8 bits per dimension, 4x compression
-- Reconstruction: original[i] = quantized[i] * scale + offset
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_828.title: Product Quantization (PQ)
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_828.content:
```sql
-- Configure PQ codebook
SELECT ruvector_pq_train(table_name::text, num_subvectors::int, bits_per_subvector::int);
-- Typical: 8 subvectors, 8 bits each = 64 bits total for 256-dim vector
```

---

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_839.title: Creating HNSW Index
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_839.content:
```sql
-- Create table with HNSW index
CREATE TABLE embeddings (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    embedding ruvector,
    metadata JSONB,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Create HNSW index with configuration
CREATE INDEX idx_embeddings_hnsw
ON embeddings USING hnsw (embedding ruvector_cosine_ops)
WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 100);
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_856.title: HNSW Parameters
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_856.content:
| Parameter | Default | Range | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------|-------------|
| `m` | 16 | 4-64 | Max connections per layer. Higher = better recall, more memory |
| `ef_construction` | 100 | 16-500 | Build-time search quality. Higher = better index, slower build |
| `ef_search` | 40 | 10-1000 | Query-time search quality. Higher = better recall, slower query |

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_864.title: Distance Operators
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_864.content:
| Operator | Function | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| `<=>` | `ruvector_cosine_ops` | Cosine distance (1 - cosine similarity) |
| `<->` | `ruvector_l2_ops` | Euclidean (L2) distance |
| `<#>` | `ruvector_ip_ops` | Negative inner product |

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_872.title: Optimal Settings by Use Case

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_894.title: Memory Estimation
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_894.content:
```
HNSW Memory = vectors * dimensions * 4 bytes + vectors * m * 8 bytes * 2
```

Example: 1M vectors, 384 dimensions, m=16:
```
= 1,000,000 * 384 * 4 + 1,000,000 * 16 * 8 * 2
= 1,536 MB + 256 MB = ~1.8 GB
```

---

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_910.title: Creating IVFFlat Index
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_910.content:
```sql
-- Create IVFFlat index
CREATE INDEX idx_embeddings_ivfflat
ON embeddings USING ivfflat (embedding ruvector_cosine_ops)
WITH (lists = 100);
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_919.title: IVFFlat Parameters
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_919.content:
| Parameter | Default | Recommended | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|-------------|
| `lists` | 100 | sqrt(n) to 4*sqrt(n) | Number of inverted lists/clusters |
| `probes` | 1 | 10-50 | Lists to search at query time |

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_935.title: Query Configuration
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_935.content:
```sql
-- Set probes for better recall
SET ivfflat.probes = 10;

-- Query with configured probes
SELECT id, embedding <=> query::ruvector AS distance
FROM embeddings
ORDER BY distance
LIMIT 10;
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_948.title: HNSW vs IVFFlat Comparison
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_948.content:
| Aspect | HNSW | IVFFlat |
|--------|------|---------|
| **Build Time** | Slower | Faster |
| **Query Speed** | Faster | Slower |
| **Memory Usage** | Higher | Lower |
| **Recall** | Higher | Lower |
| **Insertions** | Efficient | Requires rebuild |
| **Best For** | < 10M vectors | > 10M vectors |

---

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_961.title: ReasoningBank Integration
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_961.content: ReasoningBank enables self-learning capabilities that allow the database to improve search quality over time based on user feedback.

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_985.title: Enabling Self-Learning

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_997.title: Recording Feedback
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_997.content:
```sql
-- Record positive/negative feedback on search results
SELECT ruvector_record_feedback(
  'embeddings',
  query_vector,
  ARRAY[123, 456, 789],  -- relevant IDs
  ARRAY[101, 202]        -- irrelevant IDs
);
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1009.title: Using Learned Parameters

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1024.title: Trajectory Format
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1024.content:
```json
{
  "state": [0.1, 0.2, ...],      // Query context vector
  "action": "expand_hnsw",       // Action taken (ef_search increase)
  "parameters": {"ef_search": 64},
  "outcome": "success",          // Result quality
  "reward": 0.95,                // Performance score
  "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}
```

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1047.title: Reinforcement Learning Algorithms
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1047.content:
RuVector supports 9 RL algorithms for learning:
1. Q-Learning
2. SARSA
3. Decision Transformer
4. Actor-Critic
5. PPO (Proximal Policy Optimization)
6. DQN (Deep Q-Network)
7. Rainbow DQN
8. SAC (Soft Actor-Critic)
9. TD3 (Twin Delayed DDPG)

---

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1070.title: SIMD Acceleration
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1070.content:
RuVector uses SIMD instructions for ~2x performance improvement:
- AVX-512 (Intel/AMD modern CPUs)
- AVX2 (Intel/AMD older CPUs)
- NEON (ARM/Apple Silicon)

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1077.title: Benchmark Results
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1077.content:
| Operation | 10K vectors | 100K vectors | 1M vectors |
|-----------|-------------|--------------|------------|
| **HNSW Build** | 0.8s | 8.2s | 95s |
| **HNSW Search (top-10)** | 0.3ms | 0.5ms | 1.2ms |
| **Cosine Distance** | 0.01ms | 0.01ms | 0.01ms |
| **Poincare Distance** | 0.02ms | 0.02ms | 0.02ms |
| **GCN Forward** | 2.1ms | 18ms | 180ms |
| **BM25 Score** | 0.05ms | 0.08ms | 0.15ms |
| **Binary Quantization** | 0.001ms | 0.001ms | 0.001ms |
| **Scalar Quantization** | 0.002ms | 0.002ms | 0.002ms |

*Dimensions: 384 for vector ops, 128 for GNN*

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1092.title: Throughput Metrics
RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1092.content:
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Search Latency (k=10) | ~61 microseconds |
| Queries Per Second | ~16,400 QPS |
| Batch Insert Rate | 100,000+ vectors/second |

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1149.content:

RUVECTOR_POSTGRES_CLI_v0.2.6.md_1162.title: Comparison with pgvector

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_516.title: Project Setup - Local Installation (Recommended)
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_516.content:
Always install RuvNet packages **locally per project**, not globally:

```bash

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_527.title: Initialize claude-flow for the project
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_527.content:
npx claude-flow@alpha init --force
```

**Why Local?**
- **Isolation**: Each project has its own knowledge base and memory
- **Version Control**: Different projects can use different versions
- **No Pollution**: No cross-project data contamination
- **Reproducibility**: `package.json` defines exact dependencies

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_640.title: Railway Deployment (Recommended for Production)
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_640.content:
Railway supports Node.js native modules and persistent storage:

```bash

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_652.title: Deploy
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_652.content:
railway up
```

**railway.json configuration:**
```json
{
  "build": {
    "builder": "NIXPACKS",
    "buildCommand": "npm install && npm run build"
  },
  "deploy": {
    "startCommand": "npm start",
    "healthcheckPath": "/health"
  }
}
```

**Key Railway Features:**
- ✅ Node.js v22 support
- ✅ Native module compilation (node-gyp)
- ✅ Persistent volumes for `.swarm/` and `.ruvector/`
- ✅ Auto-scaling on traffic
- ✅ Zero-config Node.js detection

**Mount persistent volume for data:**
```bash
railway volume create --mount /app/.swarm
railway volume create --mount /app/.ruvector
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_690.title: Install build tools for native modules
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_690.content:
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++

WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_690.section: Install build tools for native modules

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_700.title: Security: Run as non-root
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_700.content:
RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S nodejs && adduser -S nodejs -u 1001
USER nodejs

WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder --chown=nodejs:nodejs /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --chown=nodejs:nodejs . .

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_708.title: Persistent data directories
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_708.content:
VOLUME ["/app/.swarm", "/app/.ruvector"]

EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
```

**docker-compose.yml:**
```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  app:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - swarm-data:/app/.swarm
      - ruvector-data:/app/.ruvector
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  swarm-data:
  ruvector-data:
```

**Run with:**
```bash
docker-compose up -d
```

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_740.title: Vercel Deployment (Limited - Read-Only Use Cases)
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_740.content:
**⚠️ Limitations:**
- Ephemeral storage (no persistent `.swarm/` or `.ruvector/`)
- Native module GLIBC compatibility issues
- 10-second function timeout (hobby), 60s (pro)
- No WebSocket support in serverless

**When Vercel Works:**
- Static sites with pre-built knowledge bases
- API routes that query external databases
- Read-only vector search (bundle data at build time)

**Workaround for read-only:**
```javascript
// Bundle knowledge at build time
import knowledgeBase from './data/knowledge.json';

// Create in-memory database from bundled data
const db = new ruvector.VectorDB(768);
knowledgeBase.forEach(item => db.insert(item.id, item.embedding));
```

**For Vercel, consider:**
- Use Vercel Postgres or Vercel KV for persistence
- Pre-compute embeddings at build time
- Use external vector DB (Pinecone, Weaviate Cloud)

RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_912.title: Security Considerations for Enterprise
RUVNET_ECOSYSTEM_COMPLETE.md_912.content:
1. **Network Isolation**: Run Ollama on internal network only
2. **No Telemetry**: Ollama and Qwen don't phone home
3. **Audit Logging**: Log all queries for compliance
4. **Data Encryption**: Encrypt `.swarm/` and `.ruvector/` at rest
5. **Access Control**: Use reverse proxy with authentication

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_23.title: Description
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_23.content: RvLite is an SQLite-style vector database for browsers and edge environments. It provides a unified SQL + SPARQL + Cypher query interface with zero dependencies, running anywhere JavaScript runs - browsers, Node.js, Deno, Bun, Cloudflare Workers, and Vercel Edge.

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_36.title: Core Class: `RvLite`
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_36.content:
```javascript
import { RvLite } from '@rvlite/wasm';

// Create database instance
const db = await RvLite.create(options);
```

**Constructor Options:**
| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `path` | string | `:memory:` | Database file path |
| `dimensions` | number | 384 | Default vector dimensions |
| `indexType` | string | `hnsw` | Index type (hnsw, ivfflat) |
| `m` | number | 16 | HNSW connections per layer |
| `efConstruction` | number | 64 | HNSW construction quality |

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_54.title: SQL Interface

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_79.title: SPARQL Interface

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_107.title: Cypher Interface
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_107.content:
```javascript
// Graph pattern matching
const results = await db.cypher(`
  MATCH (d:Document)-[:SIMILAR]->(related:Document)
  WHERE d.id = $docId
  RETURN related.content, related.score
  ORDER BY related.score DESC
  LIMIT 5
`, { docId: 123 });

// Create relationships
await db.cypher(`
  MATCH (a:Document), (b:Document)
  WHERE a.id = $aId AND b.id = $bId
  CREATE (a)-[:SIMILAR {score: $score}]->(b)
`, { aId: 1, bId: 2, score: 0.95 });
```

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_476.content:
server:
  host: localhost
  port: 5432
  database: vectors

extension:
  version: "0.2.6"
  simd: auto  # auto | avx512 | avx2 | neon | none

indexes:
  hnsw:
    m: 16
    ef_construction: 64
    ef_search: 100
  ivfflat:
    lists: 100
    probes: 10

learning:
  enabled: true
  trajectory_retention: 30d
  pattern_threshold: 0.7

embeddings:
  default_model: all-MiniLM-L6-v2
  cache_size: 10000
```

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_792.title: Configuration Options
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_792.content:
```javascript
const config = {
  // Storage
  path: './agent-memory.db',
  persistToDisk: true,

  // Vectors
  dimensions: 384,
  indexType: 'hnsw',  // hnsw | flat | ivf

  // HNSW Configuration
  hnswM: 16,              // Connections per layer
  hnswEf: 100,            // Dynamic candidate list size
  hnswEfConstruction: 64, // Build-time quality

  // Quantization
  quantization: 'scalar', // none | binary | scalar | product

  // Performance
  cacheSize: 1000,        // LRU cache entries
  batchSize: 100,         // Batch operation size

  // Learning
  learningEnabled: false,
  learningAlgorithm: 'decision-transformer',

  // ReasoningBank
  reasoningEnabled: false,
  reasoningConfidenceThreshold: 0.7,

  // Distributed
  quicSyncEnabled: false,
  syncEndpoint: null
};
```

**Environment Variables:**
```bash
AGENTDB_ENABLED=true
AGENTDB_PATH=.agentdb/memory.db
AGENTDB_QUANTIZATION=scalar  # binary|scalar|product|none
AGENTDB_CACHE_SIZE=1000
AGENTDB_HNSW_M=16
AGENTDB_HNSW_EF=100
AGENTDB_LEARNING=false
AGENTDB_LEARNING_ALGORITHM=decision-transformer
AGENTDB_REASONING=false
AGENTDB_QUIC_SYNC=false
```

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_885.title: Description
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_885.content: The first competitive agentic platform built entirely on MCP (Model Context Protocol). Deploy autonomous AI swarms, train neural networks, and compete in coding challenges while earning rUv credits. Features 70+ MCP tools for swarm management, sandbox execution, workflow automation, and distributed neural training.

claude mcp add ruv-swarm npx ruv-swarm@latest mcp start
```

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1493.title: Core Vector Operations (10 functions)

rv_inner_product(a ruvector, b ruvector) -> float8
rv_manhattan_distance(a ruvector, b ruvector) -> float8

-- Vector arithmetic
rv_add(a ruvector, b ruvector) -> ruvector
rv_subtract(a ruvector, b ruvector) -> ruvector
rv_multiply(v ruvector, scalar float8) -> ruvector
rv_divide(v ruvector, scalar float8) -> ruvector

rv_magnitude(v ruvector) -> float8
```

rv_log_map_poincare(base ruvector, target ruvector) -> ruvector

```

```sql
-- Core attention
rv_scaled_dot_attention(q ruvector, k ruvector, v ruvector) -> ruvector

rv_flash_attention(q ruvector, k ruvector, v ruvector) -> ruvector

-- Efficient attention variants
rv_linear_attention(q ruvector, k ruvector, v ruvector, kernel text) -> ruvector
rv_sparse_attention(q ruvector, k ruvector, v ruvector, pattern jsonb) -> ruvector
rv_performer_attention(q ruvector, k ruvector, v ruvector, features int) -> ruvector

rv_longformer_attention(q ruvector, k ruvector, v ruvector, window int) -> ruvector

rv_relative_attention(q ruvector, k ruvector, v ruvector, positions int[]) -> ruvector

-- Causal/Masked attention

rv_sliding_window_attention(q ruvector, k ruvector, v ruvector, window int) -> ruvector

-- Cross attention

rv_gated_attention(q ruvector, k ruvector, v ruvector, gate ruvector) -> ruvector

-- Graph attention

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1611.content:

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1685.title: Overview
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1685.content: RuVector implements automatic tiered storage similar to a computer's memory hierarchy. Frequently accessed vectors stay in fast storage while rarely used vectors are automatically compressed to save memory.

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1699.title: How It Works
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1699.content:
```sql
-- Vectors are automatically promoted/demoted based on access patterns
-- No manual configuration required

-- Example: Insert vector (starts in Hot tier)
INSERT INTO documents (content, embedding)
VALUES ('New document', '[0.1, 0.2, ...]'::ruvector);

-- As access frequency decreases, vector moves to cooler tiers
-- Query performance remains excellent due to intelligent caching
```

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1715.title: Format Specifications
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1715.content:
**f32 (Hot)**
- Full IEEE 754 single-precision floats
- 4 bytes per dimension
- Zero compression overhead
- Maximum precision for frequently accessed vectors

**f16 (Warm)**
- IEEE 754 half-precision floats
- 2 bytes per dimension
- 50% memory reduction
- Imperceptible accuracy loss for most use cases

**PQ8 (Cool)**
- Product Quantization with 8-bit centroids
- 256 centroids per subspace
- ~97% accuracy retention
- 8x memory reduction

**PQ4 (Cold)**
- Product Quantization with 4-bit centroids
- 16 centroids per subspace
- ~95% accuracy retention
- 16x memory reduction

**Binary (Archive)**
- 1-bit per dimension
- Hamming distance for initial filtering
- Re-ranking with full precision on final candidates
- 32x memory reduction

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1990.title: Semantic Search with PostgreSQL
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_1990.content:
```sql
-- Search patterns by semantic similarity
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION search_patterns_semantic(
    query_embedding ruvector,
    domain_filter TEXT DEFAULT NULL,
    min_confidence FLOAT8 DEFAULT 0.5,
    result_limit INT DEFAULT 10
) RETURNS TABLE (
    key TEXT,
    content TEXT,
    confidence FLOAT8,
    similarity FLOAT8
) AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
        p.key,
        p.content,
        p.confidence,
        1 - (pe.embedding <=> query_embedding) AS similarity
    FROM patterns p
    JOIN pattern_embeddings pe ON p.id = pe.pattern_id
    WHERE p.confidence >= min_confidence
      AND (domain_filter IS NULL OR p.domain = domain_filter)
    ORDER BY pe.embedding <=> query_embedding
    LIMIT result_limit;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
```

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_2029.title: docker-compose.yml
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_2029.content:
version: '3.8'

services:
  ruvector-postgres:
    image: ruvnet/ruvector-postgres:latest
    container_name: ruvector-db
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ruvector
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
      POSTGRES_DB: vectors
      # Extension configuration
      RUVECTOR_SIMD: auto  # auto | avx512 | avx2 | neon
      RUVECTOR_HNSW_M: 16
      RUVECTOR_HNSW_EF: 100
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - ruvector_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./init:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ruvector -d vectors"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 4G
        reservations:
          memory: 2G

volumes:
  ruvector_data:
```

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_2096.title: docker-compose.prod.yml
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_2096.content:
version: '3.8'

services:
  ruvector-postgres:
    image: ruvnet/ruvector-postgres:latest
    deploy:
      replicas: 1
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD_FILE: /run/secrets/db_password
    secrets:
      - db_password
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    networks:
      - ruvector-net

  agentdb:
    image: ruvnet/agentdb:latest
    deploy:
      replicas: 2
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://ruvector@ruvector-postgres:5432/vectors
      AGENTDB_QUANTIZATION: scalar
    depends_on:
      - ruvector-postgres
    networks:
      - ruvector-net

  flow-nexus:
    image: ruvnet/flow-nexus:latest
    deploy:
      replicas: 1
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://ruvector@ruvector-postgres:5432/vectors
      E2B_API_KEY_FILE: /run/secrets/e2b_key
    secrets:
      - e2b_key
    depends_on:
      - ruvector-postgres
      - agentdb
    networks:
      - ruvector-net

  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf
      - ./certs:/etc/nginx/certs
    depends_on:
      - flow-nexus
    networks:
      - ruvector-net

secrets:
  db_password:
    external: true
  e2b_key:
    external: true

volumes:
  pgdata:

networks:
  ruvector-net:
    driver: overlay
```

RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_2172.title: k8s/ruvector-deployment.yaml
RUVNET_PACKAGES_COMPLETE.md_2172.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ruvector-postgres
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: ruvector-postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: ruvector-postgres
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: ruvector-postgres
          image: ruvnet/ruvector-postgres:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: ruvector-secrets
                  key: db-password
          volumeMounts:
            - name: pgdata
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "2Gi"
              cpu: "1000m"
            limits:
              memory: "4Gi"
              cpu: "2000m"
      volumes:
        - name: pgdata
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: ruvector-pvc

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: ruvector-postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app: ruvector-postgres
  ports:
    - port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
  type: ClusterIP
```

CLAUDE.md_2.title: 🚨 CRITICAL: CONCURRENT EXECUTION & FILE MANAGEMENT
CLAUDE.md_2.content:
**ABSOLUTE RULES**:
1. ALL operations MUST be concurrent/parallel in a single message
2. **NEVER save working files, text/mds and tests to the root folder**
3. ALWAYS organize files in appropriate subdirectories
4. **USE CLAUDE CODE'S TASK TOOL** for spawning agents concurrently, not just MCP

CLAUDE.md_10.title: ⚡ GOLDEN RULE: "1 MESSAGE = ALL RELATED OPERATIONS"
CLAUDE.md_10.content:
**MANDATORY PATTERNS:**
- **TodoWrite**: ALWAYS batch ALL todos in ONE call (5-10+ todos minimum)
- **Task tool (Claude Code)**: ALWAYS spawn ALL agents in ONE message with full instructions
- **File operations**: ALWAYS batch ALL reads/writes/edits in ONE message
- **Bash commands**: ALWAYS batch ALL terminal operations in ONE message
- **Memory operations**: ALWAYS batch ALL memory store/retrieve in ONE message
CLAUDE.md_10.section: ⚡ GOLDEN RULE: "1 MESSAGE = ALL RELATED OPERATIONS"

CLAUDE.md_19.title: 🎯 CRITICAL: Claude Code Task Tool for Agent Execution
CLAUDE.md_19.content:
**Claude Code's Task tool is the PRIMARY way to spawn agents:**
```javascript
// ✅ CORRECT: Use Claude Code's Task tool for parallel agent execution
[Single Message]:
  Task("Research agent", "Analyze requirements and patterns...", "researcher")
  Task("Coder agent", "Implement core features...", "coder")
  Task("Tester agent", "Create comprehensive tests...", "tester")
  Task("Reviewer agent", "Review code quality...", "reviewer")
  Task("Architect agent", "Design system architecture...", "system-architect")
```

**MCP tools are ONLY for coordination setup:**
- `mcp__claude-flow__swarm_init` - Initialize coordination topology
- `mcp__claude-flow__agent_spawn` - Define agent types for coordination
- `mcp__claude-flow__task_orchestrate` - Orchestrate high-level workflows
CLAUDE.md_19.section: 🎯 CRITICAL: Claude Code Task Tool for Agent Execution

CLAUDE.md_37.title: 📁 File Organization Rules
CLAUDE.md_37.content:
**NEVER save to root folder. Use these directories:**
- `/src` - Source code files
- `/tests` - Test files
- `/docs` - Documentation and markdown files
- `/config` - Configuration files
- `/scripts` - Utility scripts
- `/examples` - Example code

CLAUDE.md_47.title: Project Overview
CLAUDE.md_47.content: This project uses SPARC (Specification, Pseudocode, Architecture, Refinement, Completion) methodology with Claude-Flow orchestration for systematic Test-Driven Development.

CLAUDE.md_53.title: Core Commands
CLAUDE.md_53.content:
- `npx claude-flow sparc modes` - List available modes
- `npx claude-flow sparc run <mode> "<task>"` - Execute specific mode
- `npx claude-flow sparc tdd "<feature>"` - Run complete TDD workflow
- `npx claude-flow sparc info <mode>` - Get mode details

CLAUDE.md_59.title: Batchtools Commands
CLAUDE.md_59.content:
- `npx claude-flow sparc batch <modes> "<task>"` - Parallel execution
- `npx claude-flow sparc pipeline "<task>"` - Full pipeline processing
- `npx claude-flow sparc concurrent <mode> "<tasks-file>"` - Multi-task processing

CLAUDE.md_64.title: Build Commands
CLAUDE.md_64.content:
- `npm run build` - Build project
- `npm run test` - Run tests
- `npm run lint` - Linting
- `npm run typecheck` - Type checking

CLAUDE.md_70.title: SPARC Workflow Phases
CLAUDE.md_70.content:
1. **Specification** - Requirements analysis (`sparc run spec-pseudocode`)
2. **Pseudocode** - Algorithm design (`sparc run spec-pseudocode`)
3. **Architecture** - System design (`sparc run architect`)
4. **Refinement** - TDD implementation (`sparc tdd`)
5. **Completion** - Integration (`sparc run integration`)

CLAUDE.md_78.title: Code Style & Best Practices
CLAUDE.md_78.content:
- **Modular Design**: Files under 500 lines
- **Environment Safety**: Never hardcode secrets
- **Test-First**: Write tests before implementation
- **Clean Architecture**: Separate concerns
- **Documentation**: Keep updated

CLAUDE.md_117.title: Claude Code Handles ALL EXECUTION:
CLAUDE.md_117.content:
- **Task tool**: Spawn and run agents concurrently for actual work
- File operations (Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Glob, Grep)
- Code generation and programming
- Bash commands and system operations
- Implementation work
- Project navigation and analysis
- TodoWrite and task management
- Git operations
- Package management
- Testing and debugging

CLAUDE.md_129.title: MCP Tools ONLY COORDINATE:
CLAUDE.md_129.content:
- Swarm initialization (topology setup)
- Agent type definitions (coordination patterns)
- Task orchestration (high-level planning)
- Memory management
- Neural features
- Performance tracking
- GitHub integration

**KEY**: MCP coordinates the strategy, Claude Code's Task tool executes with real agents.

CLAUDE.md_143.title: Add MCP servers (Claude Flow required, others optional)
CLAUDE.md_143.content:
claude mcp add claude-flow npx claude-flow@alpha mcp start
claude mcp add ruv-swarm npx ruv-swarm mcp start  # Optional: Enhanced coordination
claude mcp add flow-nexus npx flow-nexus@latest mcp start  # Optional: Cloud features
```
CLAUDE.md_143.section: Add MCP servers (Claude Flow required, others optional)

CLAUDE.md_166.title: Flow-Nexus MCP Tools (Optional Advanced Features)
CLAUDE.md_166.content:
Flow-Nexus extends MCP capabilities with 70+ cloud-based orchestration tools:

**Key MCP Tool Categories:**
- **Swarm & Agents**: `swarm_init`, `swarm_scale`, `agent_spawn`, `task_orchestrate`
- **Sandboxes**: `sandbox_create`, `sandbox_execute`, `sandbox_upload` (cloud execution)
- **Templates**: `template_list`, `template_deploy` (pre-built project templates)
- **Neural AI**: `neural_train`, `neural_patterns`, `seraphina_chat` (AI assistant)
- **GitHub**: `github_repo_analyze`, `github_pr_manage` (repository management)
- **Real-time**: `execution_stream_subscribe`, `realtime_subscribe` (live monitoring)
- **Storage**: `storage_upload`, `storage_list` (cloud file management)

**Authentication Required:**
- Register: `mcp__flow-nexus__user_register` or `npx flow-nexus@latest register`
- Login: `mcp__flow-nexus__user_login` or `npx flow-nexus@latest login`
- Access 70+ specialized MCP tools for advanced orchestration

CLAUDE.md_185.title: The Correct Pattern:
CLAUDE.md_185.content:
1. **Optional**: Use MCP tools to set up coordination topology
2. **REQUIRED**: Use Claude Code's Task tool to spawn agents that do actual work
3. **REQUIRED**: Each agent runs hooks for coordination
4. **REQUIRED**: Batch all operations in single messages

CLAUDE.md_192.title: Example Full-Stack Development:
CLAUDE.md_192.content:
```javascript
// Single message with all agent spawning via Claude Code's Task tool
[Parallel Agent Execution]:
  Task("Backend Developer", "Build REST API with Express. Use hooks for coordination.", "backend-dev")
  Task("Frontend Developer", "Create React UI. Coordinate with backend via memory.", "coder")
  Task("Database Architect", "Design PostgreSQL schema. Store schema in memory.", "code-analyzer")
  Task("Test Engineer", "Write Jest tests. Check memory for API contracts.", "tester")
  Task("DevOps Engineer", "Setup Docker and CI/CD. Document in memory.", "cicd-engineer")
  Task("Security Auditor", "Review authentication. Report findings via hooks.", "reviewer")
  
  // All todos batched together
  TodoWrite { todos: [...8-10 todos...] }
  
  // All file operations together
  Write "backend/server.js"
  Write "frontend/App.jsx"
  Write "database/schema.sql"
```

CLAUDE.md_215.title: Every Agent Spawned via Task Tool MUST:
CLAUDE.md_215.content:
**1️⃣ BEFORE Work:**
```bash
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks pre-task --description "[task]"
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks session-restore --session-id "swarm-[id]"
```

**2️⃣ DURING Work:**
```bash
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks post-edit --file "[file]" --memory-key "swarm/[agent]/[step]"
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks notify --message "[what was done]"
```

**3️⃣ AFTER Work:**
```bash
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks post-task --task-id "[task]"
npx claude-flow@alpha hooks session-end --export-metrics true
```
CLAUDE.md_215.section: Every Agent Spawned via Task Tool MUST:

DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_0.title: RuvNet Ecosystem Deployment Guide
DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_0.content:
> **Comprehensive deployment documentation for the RuvNet AI ecosystem**
> **Version**: 1.0.0
> **Last Updated**: December 2025

---

DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_20.title: Overview
DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_20.content:
The RuvNet ecosystem consists of several interconnected packages that work together to provide AI-powered vector search, LLM orchestration, and agent coordination:

| Package | Purpose | Deployment Considerations |
|---------|---------|---------------------------|
| `ruvector` | Vector database with HNSW indexing | Native bindings, WASM fallback |
| `@ruvector/ruvllm` | Self-learning LLM orchestration | SONA engine, FastGRNN router |
| `agentic-flow` | 150+ AI agents, 213 MCP tools | ReasoningBank memory |
| `claude-flow` | Enterprise orchestration | AgentDB, hive-mind coordination |
| `@ruvector/postgres-cli` | PostgreSQL vector extension | Docker or native installation |

DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_32.title: Deployment Platform Comparison
DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_32.content:
| Platform | Native Modules | Persistent Storage | GPU Support | Best For |
|----------|---------------|-------------------|-------------|----------|
| **Docker** | Full support | Volumes | nvidia-docker | Enterprise/Self-hosted |
| **Railway** | Full support | Volumes | Limited | Full-stack apps |
| **Kubernetes** | Full support | PVCs | GPU nodes | Large-scale production |
| **Air-gapped** | Full support | Local storage | Full | Secure environments |

---

DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_51.title: Run with persistent storage
DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_51.content:
docker run -d --name ruvector-pg \
  -e POSTGRES_USER=ruvector \
  -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=secret \
  -e POSTGRES_DB=ruvector \
  -p 5432:5432 \
  -v ruvector_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data \
  ruvnet/ruvector-postgres:latest
```

DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_61.title: Full Application docker-compose.yml
DEPLOYMENT-GUIDE.md_61.content:
```yaml
version: '3.8'

services:
  # RuVector PostgreSQL with vector extensions
  ruvector-postgres:
    image: ruvnet/ruvector-postgres:latest
    container_name: ruvector-pg
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-ruvector}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-secret}
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-ruvector}
      # Performance tuning
      POSTGRES_SHARED_BUFFERS: 256MB
      POSTGRES_EFFECTIVE_CACHE_SIZE: 768MB
      POSTGRES_WORK_MEM: 64MB
    ports:
      - "5432:5432"
    volumes:
      - ruvector_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./init-scripts:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-ruvector} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-ruvector}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - ruvnet-network

  # Application server
  app:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: ruvnet-app
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: production
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://${POSTGRES_USER:-ruvector}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-secret}@ruvector-postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-ruvector}
      # API Keys (mount via secrets in production)
      ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      OPENROUTER_API_KEY: ${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      # Persistent memory and knowledge bases
      - swarm_data:/app/.swarm
      - ruvector_kb:/app/.ruvector
      - hive_mind:/app/.hive-mind
    depends_on:
      ruvector-postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 60s
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - ruvnet-network

  # Redis for caching and session management (optional)
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: ruvnet-redis
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes --maxmemory 256mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    restart: unless-stopped
    networks:
      - ruvnet-network

networks:
  ruvnet-network:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  ruvector_data:
    driver: local
  swarm_data:
    driver: local
  ruvector_kb:
    driver: local
  hive_mind:
    driver: local
  redis_data:
    driver: local
```
